verdict: FAIL — week-10 time budget 56 min is 37.8% below target (hard-fail threshold ±25%). One cross-week concept duplication (accountability gap in both 9.11 and 10.3 concepts_introduced). Five topic manifests carry stale or null expected_delivery_minutes. All other checks pass.

prerequisite_check.note: All 18 topics have prerequisites pointing to strictly earlier (week_num, position_in_module) ordering. No forward references detected.

findings.detail: accountability gap appears in concepts_introduced for both topic 9.11 and topic 10.3. The term is introduced in 9.11 (high-stakes domain context) and then re-listed as newly introduced in 10.3 (loan approval case study). 10.3 should list it as a prerequisite reference, not as a new concept_introduced. No textual contradiction exists — both uses are consistent — but the metadata signals a duplication.
findings.action_required: In sequence.index.json, remove 'accountability gap' from 10.3 concepts_introduced and add topic 9.11 as a prerequisite of 10.3 (or ensure 9.11 is already listed, which it is).

findings.detail: calibration appears in three contexts: 9.1 corpus (in plain language about AI output evaluation), 9.5 concepts_introduced as 'calibrated trust', and 10.4 as 'trust calibration'. All three uses are semantically consistent and progressively specific. No contradiction.
Introduced in 9.6 concepts_introduced. Reused in 10.1 concepts_introduced as a plain-language term (no redefinition). Consistent use.

9.5 defines automation bias (trusting automation over judgment). 10.4 defines automation complacency (reduced vigilance from correct performance). The 10.4 corpus explicitly distinguishes these two terms. Terminology is consistent and non-contradictory.

check_2_prerequisite_ordering.note: All 18 topics verified. Every prerequisite ID has a strictly lower (week_num, position_in_module) than the topic referencing it. No violations.

topics.source: reading.meta.json (1530w, ceil=8) — meta records 7 (rounding error in meta), manifest null (stale)
reading.meta.json (1556w/7min) — manifest says 8 (minor discrepancy, meta=7, formula=8)

weeks.notes: 56 min is 37.8% below the 90-min target. Hard-fail floor is 67.5 min. Week-10 is 11.5 min short of even the hard-fail floor. Only 7 topics span week 10 vs 11 in week 9. All 7 topics are standard (7–8 min) or deep (10 min) profiles; none are atomic. The budget shortfall is structural: week 10 has fewer topics and none of the standard topics were pushed to deep. Remediation options: (a) elevate 10.2 or 10.3 from standard to deep (adds ~3 min each), (b) check whether 10.2 word count (1556w) should yield 8 min via ceiling formula — correcting the meta rounding adds 1 min, (c) consider adding a topic to week 10 if curriculum permits.

check_4_no_duplication.method: Jaccard similarity on concepts_introduced sets, cross-week pairs only (within-week duplication handled by module-drift critic)

notable_near_misses.note: Shared term is a prerequisite cross-reference, not a redefinition. No action required.

accountability gap appears in both concepts_introduced lists. Flagged under Check 1 as a metadata duplication. Conceptual content differs: 9.11 introduces the term in the context of high-stakes domains; 10.3 uses it in a case-study context. Jaccard on full concept sets is below threshold, but the metadata duplication should be resolved.
automation bias (9.5) and automation complacency (10.4) are related but distinct concepts. Corpora treat them as separate. No duplication.

check_4_no_duplication.finding: No cross-week topic pair exceeds the 0.55 Jaccard threshold on Core Concepts concept sets. One metadata-level duplication (accountability gap in concepts_introduced of both 9.11 and 10.3) is flagged under Check 1.

complete — 9.5 introduces automation bias formally; 10.4 extends to automation complacency (the downstream cognitive effect)

blocking_failures.detail: Week 10 reading budget 56 min is 37.8% below the 90-min target. Hard-fail floor is 67.5 min (25% below target). Blocks course_drift_passed=true for all 7 week-10 topics.
blocking_failures.remediation: Option A: Elevate 10.2 (medical triage) from standard to deep profile — expands reading from 7→10 min, adds ~3 min. Option B: Elevate 10.3 (loan approval) from standard to deep — same gain. Doing both (A+B) brings week 10 to 62 min — still below hard-fail floor. Option C: Elevate 10.5 or 10.6 to deep (adds 3 min each). Combining A+B+C yields 68 min — just above hard-fail floor. Option D (preferred if curriculum allows): add a summary/synthesis topic to week 10 as a standard 7-min topic, bringing total to 63 min + any elevations.

non_blocking_findings.detail: accountability gap listed in concepts_introduced for both topics. Should be removed from 10.3 concepts_introduced in sequence.index.json.
